summary: Heavily-obfuscated bulk email/SMS spam toolkit with webmail session hijack and OAuth harvesting
details: Package self-describes as 'Advanced email sender' with keywords including 'red-team' and 'smtp', and ships ~40 files under.ad/ that are uniformly packed with obfuscator.io (string-array + RC4 + self-defending wrappers) — whole-tarball obfuscation designed to evade scanners. Decoded behavior includes: (1) a `nolimit web` subcommand that writes a Chrome MV3 extension to ~/.nolimit-x-ext, launches Chrome with --load-extension, opens a localhost WebSocket on port 9227, and pushes INJECT payloads invoking chrome.scripting.executeScript against the operator's signed-in Gmail / Outlook / Yahoo Mail tabs to convert authenticated webmail sessions into a mass-mail relay; (2) `nolimit auth` and `nolimit extract` subcommands that run Microsoft device-code OAuth and Google OAuth flows requesting Mail.Read / Contacts.Read / People.Read / SMTP.Send / offline_access, persist access/refresh/graph tokens to smtps.txt, and enumerate Graph /me/mailFolders/sentitems/messages or IMAP Sent folders to scrape contacts; (3) SMS provider integrations against api.telnyx.com, messaging.bandwidth.com, and rest.messagebird.com for bulk SMS spam; (4) a license-activation flow that POSTs hwid + hostname + platform to an obfuscated, undisclosed LICENSE_SERVER (.ad/x4.js) with a 6-hour re-verify loop. The package is a phishing/spam operations toolkit shipped through the npm supply chain; the comprehensive obfuscation across non-vendor source has no legitimate engineering justification and is itself a hostile signal toward downstream consumers and registry scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
